narrative:
On July 22, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 40.9%, with 1,007 out of 2,464 scheduled cases heard across 44 judges. This marks a decrease in overall hearing efficiency compared to recent court days, down from 46.6% on July 21 (1,122 of 2,407 heard) and 44.5% on July 20 (1,165 of 2,620 heard).

Judge performance varied significantly across court halls. M. Nagaprasanna in Court Hall 14 processed the highest volume of heard matters, converting 99 of 165 scheduled cases (60.0%). Shivashankar Amarannavar in Court Hall 20 achieved a strong 70.4% conversion rate by hearing 57 of 81 cases on his primary list, while V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21 heard 55 of 88 cases (62.5%). Conversely, S. Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19 scheduled a heavy caseload of 195 matters but cleared only 39 (20.0%).

By hearing stage, cases listed for "Orders" formed the largest share of the schedule, with 398 of 855 cases heard (46.5%), down from a 52.4% conversion rate on July 21. "Admission" matters saw a 43.6% hearing rate (161 of 369), whereas "Final Hearing" matters experienced lower throughput at 20.1% (96 of 478 heard), dropping slightly from 23.7% on the previous day. 

Among case types, Writ Petitions (WP) represented the largest share of the docket, with 423 of 1,126 cases heard (37.6%), compared to 41.5% on July 21. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) recorded a 42.4% clearance rate (167 of 394), while Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) saw higher engagement with 123 of 244 cases heard (50.4%).
